title: glyceria plicata subsp. spicata
intro: Glyceria plicata subsp. spicata, also known as Spiked Glyceria or Glyceria Spicata, is a type of grassy plant in the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help soothe and calm digestive issues, reduce fever, and aid in wound healing. The health benefits of Glyceria plicata subsp. spicata include its potential to alleviate digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions made from its leaves and stems, which can be used as teas or applied topically as poultices or compresses.
